task_completion.description: Open the NCH Copper Point of Sale application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NCH Copper Point of Sale application main window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the tax configuration settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Options or Settings dialog is open, and the 'Tax' or 'Taxes' tab/section is actively selected.
The text 'TX Sales Tax' is typed into the tax name or description input field.
The value '8.25' is typed into the tax rate percentage input field.

task_completion.description: Save the configuration and verify it appears in the tax settings list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The newly created tax 'TX Sales Tax' with a rate of '8.25%' is visible as a saved entry in the list of configured taxes within the settings.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to configure the tax
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the NCH Copper POS GUI menus and dialogs, rather than using a terminal or command prompt to execute scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine NCH Copper POS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used is genuinely NCH Copper Point of Sale, not a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like Notepad) open editing files in the AppData directory to manually inject the tax rate.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected tax name is exactly 'TX Sales Tax' and the expected tax rate is '8.25' (or 8.25%).
